findings.title: Extended-mode regex comments corrupt Rust backreference numbering
findings.claim: Rust's numeric-backreference rewrite understands `(?#...)` comments but not `#...` line comments under `(?x)`. Parentheses in an extended-mode comment are counted as capture groups, so a valid Python-dialect pattern compiles with silently changed semantics and breaks target attribution parity.
findings.reproduction: Used valid v2 facts containing one T frame at path `abb` with value 1, and target category Hit with a pattern consisting of `regex:(?x)# (`, a newline, then `(?P<a>a)(?P<b>b)` followed by numeric backreference 2. Ran `.venv/bin/clankerprof targets --facts ... --config ...` and `target/debug/clankerprof-rs targets --facts ... --config ...`. Python exited 0 with `categories[0].name = Hit`; Rust exited 0 with `categories[0].name = Other`. Python treats the parenthesis after `#` as comment text, while Rust counts it at targets.rs:501 and rewrites backreference 2 to group `a`. The documented engine exceptions do not include this compiler-induced semantic change.

findings.title: Scope retention still erases nonzero caller/leaf subtrees
findings.claim: The R8 subtree fix considers leaf-function totals but still ignores caller/leaf evidence when deciding whether categories and domains are renderable. Signed caller pairs can therefore cancel at the coarser key and disappear completely, violating the every-level rendered-subtree rule.
findings.reproduction: First ran both scope CLIs with samples `[Leaf, CallerA, Boundary]=+10` and `[Leaf, CallerB, Boundary]=-10`, cost kind Work matching Leaf, and scope Scope matching Boundary. Both exited 0 with `boundaries[0].buckets = []`, although the omitted category had caller pairs `CallerA -> Leaf = 10` and `CallerB -> Leaf = -10`. A second fixture used leaves LeafA (+10) and LeafB (-10) under one Owner and configured domain D matching Owner. Both CLIs emitted a Work category with `time_ns: 0`, leaf functions `LeafA: 10` and `LeafB: -10`, and caller pairs `Owner -> LeafA: 10` and `Owner -> LeafB: -10`, but emitted `domains: []`. The filters at render.py:213-219/224-230 and scopes.rs:748-760/786-792 omit caller-pair maps that the renderers later expose.

findings.title: Core-class categorization bypasses the rule pack's native-path matcher
findings.claim: Core-class handling manually checks pseudo paths and positive native markers instead of calling `is_native_path`. It consequently ignores `native_path_patterns` and also bypasses native-path exclusions, in both implementations.
findings.reproduction: Used an Object#new leaf at `/native/ext.so`, core-class CSV `Object`, an empty category map for parent T, and a runtime pack with `native_path_patterns: [regex:/native/]` plus `core_native_default_category: Native Core`. Both target CLIs exited 0 and emitted category `Other` for all 10 units. A direct Python probe printed exactly `is_native_path=True category=None`. Conversely, with marker `/native/`, exclusion marker `/native/app/`, and path `/native/app/object.rb`, both CLIs emitted `Native Core` even though `is_native_path` returns false. The core branch at categorize.py:189-195 and categorize.rs:129-136 checks only positive markers.

findings.title: A configured `(all)` slice collides with the implicit fallback
findings.claim: `(all)` is the implicit unmatched-sample pseudo-slice but is not reserved during slice validation. A non-default path slice with that name is silently merged with the fallback, absorbing samples that do not match its paths while retaining misleading configured metadata.
findings.reproduction: Used two valid samples: `/special/a.rb` with value 10 and `/other/b.rb` with value 20. The slice file contained one non-default slice named `(all)`, paths `[/special/**]`, and metadata `intended: special-only`. Both Python and Rust exited 0 and emitted one `(all)` row with `time_ns: 30`, `pct: 100.0`, both frames, and the `special-only` metadata. The path rule only matches 10 units; the unmatched 20 units are merged because both implementations use the same string for the configured row and implicit fallback. Validation reserves only `(gc)` and `(uncollapsible)`, despite the plan identifying `(all)` as a pseudo-slice.

findings.title: Facts v2 import does not enforce zero-based sample indexes
findings.claim: Both strict facts-v2 importers merely range-check `sample_index`; they do not require it to equal the sample's position. Duplicate or non-zero-start indexes therefore violate the normative stable zero-based profile order while being accepted as valid facts.
findings.reproduction: Created valid v2 facts with two samples whose `sample_index` fields were both 7, each contributing value 1 to frame T, with a self-consistent summary. Both target CLIs exited 0 and emitted a normal report with `total_time_ns: 2`, category `Other`, and `samples: 2`. The expected indexes for this ordered array are 0 and 1. Python assigns `_sample_index(entry)` without comparing it to the loop position at facts.py:291-309; Rust does the same at facts.rs:325-355.

summary: Five new defects were independently reproduced: four major semantic/attribution failures and one minor facts-validation gap. `./bin/dev check` passed, and the Rust workspace tests passed again through `./bin/dev test-rust`; a direct standalone `cargo test --workspace` invocation was blocked only by the read-only sandbox denying Cargo's build-lock write. The tracker reports 71/71 baseline requirements done.
